statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.

hints.text: Check the condition for dequeueing an element

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: bool solve(vector<int>& q) { if (q.empty()) return true; if (q.size() == 1) { q.pop_back(); return q.empty(); } int front = q.front(); q.pop_front(); if (front == q.front()) return false; q.push_back(front); return true; } };
starterCode.java: class Solution { public boolean solve(int[] q) { if (q.length == 0) return true; if (q.length == 1) { q = remove(0); if (q.length == 0) return true; } int front = q[0]; q = remove(0); if (front == q[0]) return false; q = add(front, q); return true; }   private int[] remove(int index) { int[] result = new int[q.length - 1]; System.arraycopy(q, 0, result, 0, index); System.arraycopy(q, index + 1, result, index, q.length - index - 1); return result; } private int[] add(int element, int[] q) { int[] result = new int[q.length + 1]; for (int i = 0; i < q.length; ++i) result[i] = q[i]; result[q.length] = element; return result; } }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def solve(self, q: list[int]) -> bool: if not q: return True if len(q) == 1: q.pop(0) front = q[0] q.pop(0) if front == q[0]: return False q.append(front) return True
starterCode.javascript: function solve(q) { if (q.length == 0) return true; if (q.length == 1) { q.shift(); if (q.length == 0) return true; } var front = q[0]; q.shift(); if (front == q[0]) return false; q.push(front); return true; }
